title: Make github-observe repository identifiers explicit
goal: Replace the ambiguous github-observe --repo input with explicit machine-readable repository identifier modes so a Bureau resource ID can never reach gh as a malformed OWNER/REPO slug.

acceptance.assertion: github-observe exposes mutually exclusive `--repo-slug OWNER/REPO` and `--repo-resource RESOURCE_ID` options. Legacy `--repo` is either removed or retained only through an explicit deprecation path with deterministic diagnostics.
`--repo-slug heimgewebe/bureau` succeeds and preserves the current read-only observer result; `--repo-slug repo.bureau` is rejected locally before gh runs with code unsupported-repository-slug.

`--repo-resource repo.bureau` resolves through authoritative Registry repository metadata to heimgewebe/bureau. Missing or ambiguous mappings fail locally with stable codes and never delegate the raw resource ID to gh.
Repository resources gain or reference an explicit GitHub slug field; resolution does not guess from local directory names and does not treat a mutable git remote as Registry authority.
JSON output distinguishes unsupported slug shape, missing Registry resource, missing GitHub mapping, ambiguous mapping, GitHub transport failure and observer binding findings with stable codes.

Tests cover the two successful modes, repo.bureau passed incorrectly as a slug, unknown resource ID, missing mapping, ambiguous options and unavailable GitHub transport without changing task, queue, merge or deployment authority.

metadata.observed_success: bureau --root <clean-root> --json github-observe --repo heimgewebe/bureau returned healthy=true and binding_healthy=true.
metadata.observed_failure: bureau --root <clean-root> --json github-observe --repo repo.bureau delegated the value to gh, which rejected it because OWNER/REPO was required.
metadata.deduplication: Existing observer tasks own binding observation and guards. No existing task owns the CLI identifier split or authoritative Registry-to-GitHub mapping.
does_not_establish: permission to broaden accepted identifier forms
metadata.sequencing_reason: T014 is independent of Lease Contract v2 and therefore intentionally has no dependency on T011. Artificial sequencing would reduce safe parallelism without protecting a shared file, component, process or deploy path.
metadata.path_contract: Absolute path leases remain intentional host-local effect identities. T014 improves portable repository naming by adding typed Registry-to-GitHub mapping, not by weakening path identity.
metadata.hash_semantics: T014 is planned and therefore has no terminal verification hashes. Its future execution and closeout must bind the implementation head, diff, tests and current plan hash.
